cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional gymnastics from parenting appear to boost cognitive health.

cards.title: The one change that worked: I banned social media
cards.body: A psychotherapist shares how limiting phone usage improved her mood and family relationships. Setting strict boundaries on screen time created happier children and calmer parenting.

cards.title: Think Your Teen Is Being Disrespectful? Think Again
cards.body: Understanding how to respond to teenage attitude and behavior shifts perspective. Reframing disrespect as developmental change improves parent-teen communication.

cards.title: How to Respond When Adult Child Says 'You Ruined My Life'
cards.body: When adult children blame parents for everything, apologizing and defending often backfires. Effective strategies exist for navigating these difficult conversations.

cards.title: Why Immigrant Children Feel Pressured to Preserve Culture
cards.body: Many immigrant children bear sole responsibility for maintaining family culture. Sharing this responsibility supports better identity development and well-being.

cards.title: Vapes to have less enticing names to protect children
cards.body: UK plans to stop vape companies using appealing flavor names that attract young people. Consultation underway on protecting children from nicotine experimentation.

cards.title: Pioneering treatment saves identical twins from rare condition
cards.body: Identical twins Nancy and Margo received world-first in-womb treatment for a rare pregnancy condition. The procedure was performed as part of a medical trial.

cards.title: Leadership and Life Lessons From My Father
cards.body: Good parenting builds essential leadership and life skills in children. Paternal influence shapes how children develop competence and confidence.

cards.title: Maternal Ambivalence: A Peculiar Kind of Ambivalence
cards.body: Ambivalence about motherhood has been misunderstood and portrayed negatively for too long. Understanding maternal ambivalence reveals complex, honest parenting realities.

cards.title: The Asterisk Version of Family
cards.body: Families created through double donation face unique emotional complexities and hidden losses. These families must navigate questions and identity work others rarely consider.
